title: Geoeconomic Monitor: Changing With The Times
text:
Geoeconomic Monitor: Changing With The Times

Tom Holland, Tom Miller

Gavekal Research | 12 Jun 2026

Despite the failure so far of the US and Iran to agree an interim peace deal that would allow energy exports through the Strait of Hormuz to resume, the price of oil has dropped to a three-month low. Part of this fall is attributable to short term trading on expectations that a truce is imminent. But as Tom Holland details, the longer-term softness reflects the oil market's ability in absorbing the Hormuz supply shock without major global economic disruption. In other news, Tom Miller examines the state of tri-party trade talks ahead of the upcoming review of USMCA and considers the consequences of different probable outcomes.In this issue:
